codeReview.summary: The feature updates packages/web/package.json to vite ^7.0.0 and the commit removes the old web-specific vite lock entries, but the final lockfile is left out of sync with the package manifest after the worker edited package.json back from ^7.3.1 to ^7.0.0 without rerunning bun install. That means the implementation does not fully satisfy the requested dependency-update workflow.

issues.description: The committed lockfile still records the web workspace's vite spec as `^7.3.1`, while `packages/web/package.json:29` declares `^7.0.0`. The transcript shows the worker ran `bun update vite`, then manually changed package.json back to `^7.0.0` and committed without rerunning `bun install`, so the lockfile is not synchronized with the final manifest and the requested install/update step was not fully completed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state is stale after this feature: AGENTS.md still says the web package uses vite 6.x without issues, but the reviewed change upgrades packages/web to vite ^7.0.0.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/ashishhuddar/.factory/missions/8d555b3e-2948-4f0c-abd1-2fe8dc0952a9/AGENTS.md:59 says "The web package uses vite 6.x without issues"; /Users/ashishhuddar/scopepm/packages/web/package.json:29 now declares "vite": "^7.0.0".

summary: Review failed. The code change fixes the package manifest, but the committed bun.lock still reflects `^7.3.1` for the web workspace instead of the final `^7.0.0` manifest, so the dependency update was not carried through cleanly to the lockfile.
